perturbation.original_text: The Client will hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose, copy, reproduce or distribute it to any person other than its Authorised Recipients (defined as the Client ’s directors, officers, employees, associates, affiliates and advisers who are directly concerned with the Transaction and whose knowledge o f such Confidential Information is necessary for the Transaction ).
perturbation.changed_text: The Client will endeavor to hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose, copy, reproduce or distribute it to any person other than its Authorised Recipients (defined as the Client ’s directors, officers, employees, associates, affiliates and advisers who are reasonably believed to be concerned with the Transaction and whose knowledge o f such Confidential Information is somehow necessary for the Transaction ).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will hold in strict confidence' to 'will endeavor to hold in confidence' weakens the obligation. Also, changes to the definition of 'Authorized Recipients' weaken the constraint on who can receive information, and adds subjective interpretation.
perturbation.law_explanation: German law requires good faith in contractual obligations. 'Endeavoring' is not a defined term and does not necessarily fulfill the 'good faith' requirement of § 241 para. 2 BGB. The vague wording creates ambiguity about the level of effort required, making it difficult to enforce compliance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 241 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 241 Pflichten aus dem Schuldverhältnis (1) Kraft des Schuldverhältnisses ist der Gläubiger berechtigt, von dem Schuldner eine Leistung zu fordern. Die Leistung kann auch in einem Unterlassen bestehen. (2) Das Schuldverhältnis kann nach seinem Inhalt jeden Teil zur Rücksicht auf die Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen des anderen Teils verpflichten. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

(2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im zweiten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... (2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... merken § 241 Pflichten aus dem Schuldverhältnis (1) 1 Kraft des Schuldverhältnisses ist der Gläubiger berechtigt, von dem Schuldner eine Leistung zu fordern. 2 Die Leistung kann auch in einem Unterlassen bestehen. (2) Das Schuldverhältnis kann nach seinem Inhalt jeden Teil zur Rücksicht auf die Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen des anderen Teils verpflichten. Fassung aufgrund des Gesetzes zur Modernisierung des Schuldrechts vom 26.11.2001 ( BGBl. I S. 3138 ) , in Kraft getreten am 01.01.2002 Gesetzesbegründung verfügbar Vorherige Gesetzesfassung Änderung
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will hold in strict confidence' to 'will endeavor to hold in confidence' weakens the obligation to protect confidential information. This, along with the modification to the definition of 'Authorized Recipients,' introduces ambiguity. According to the LLM's Law Explanation, German law requires good faith in contractual obligations, and 'endeavoring' may not fulfill this requirement, creating ambiguity about the required effort. The first snippet, § 241 BGB, states in (2) that the Schuldverhältnis (contractual relationship) can obligate each party to consider the rights, legal goods, and interests of the other party. By weakening the obligation to maintain confidentiality and making the definition of Authorized Recipients more subjective, the modified text contradicts the requirement to consider the interests of the other party. This aligns with the Ambiguities Legal Contradiction definition, where an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce under state or national law.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the Client nor the Authorised Recipients will, without the prior consent of Vladi Private Islands: a. reveal to any person other than an Authorised Recipient that negotiations are taking place in relation to the Transaction or reveal any other details concerning the status of negotiations b. use the Confidential Information for any purpose other t han to evaluate the Transaction
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Client nor the Authorised Recipients should, without attempting to obtain prior consent of Vladi Private Islands: a. reveal to any person that negotiations might be taking place in relation to the Transaction or reveal certain other details concerning the general status of negotiations b. use the Confidential Information for purposes other than something related to evaluating the Transaction
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will' to 'should' weakens the obligation to obtain consent. Also, changing 'are taking place' to 'might be taking place,' 'any other details' to 'certain other details,' and 'any purpose' to 'purposes other than something related to' creates ambiguity and reduces the scope of the restriction.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text imposes clear restrictions to prevent any disclosures or any unapproved use of confidential information. These obligations are clear in their intent to ensure strict confidentiality and compliance. By contrast, the modified text weakens those obligations. 'Should' creates discretion. 'Might be taking place' softens the statement of fact. The introduction of these terms runs counter to the requirement to ensure that the contract is upheld in good faith, since they open pathways for breaches to occur. The introduction of these terms undermines the clarity of the initial agreement, weakening its effectiveness and reliability, violating § 241 para. 2 of the German Civil Code.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: entitled to exchange the money deposited for suitable securities and the securities deposited for other suitable securities or for money. table of contents Section 236 Book-entry securities A book-entry security against the Federal Government or a Land may be provided as security only up to the amount of three quarters of the market value of the securities the delivery of which the creditor may demand in return for cancellation of their claim registered in the debt book. table of contents Section 237 Movable things A movable thing may be provided as security only up to the amount of two thirds of its estimated value. Things may be rejected as security if there is the concern that they will spoil or if their safekeeping entails special difficulties. table of contents Section 238 Mortgages, land charges and annuity land charges (1) A mortgage claim, a land charge or an annuity land charge is suitable as a means of providing security only if it corresponds to the prerequisites to be met for the investment of money held in trust for a ward in mortgage claims, land charges or annuity land charges at the place at which security is provided. (2) A claim secured by a debt-securing mortgage is not suitable as a means of providing security. table of contents Section 239 Surety (1) A surety is qualified if they possess assets appropriate for the amount of security to be provided and if they have their general place of jurisdiction within the territory of Germany. (2) The declaration of suretyship must contain a waiver of the defence of failure to pursue remedies. table of contents Section 240 Duty to supplement security If the security provided becomes insufficient without this being the fault of the person entitled, then it is to be supplemented or security is to be provided in some other manner. table of contents Book 2 Law of obligations table of contents Division 1 Subject matter of obligations table of contents Title 1 Duty to render performance table of contents Section 241 Duties arising from an obligation (1) By virtue of an obligation, an obligee is entitled to claim performance from the obligor. The performance may also consist of forbearance. (2) By its contents, an obligation may oblige each party to take account of the rights, legal interests and other interests of the other party. table of contents Section 241a Unsolicited performance (1) The supply of movable things that are not being sold based on measures of compulsory enforcement or other judicial measures (goods), or the provision of other services to the consumer by a trader, does not give rise to a claim against the consumer if the consumer has not ordered these goods or other services. (2) Statutory claims are not excluded if the performance was not intended for the recipient or was made in the mistaken belief that there had been an order, and the recipient was aware of this or could have been aware of this had they exercised the care required in business dealings. (3) There may be no derogation from the stipulations of this provision to the disadvantage of the consumer. The stipulations apply even if they are circumvented by other arrangements. table of contents Section 242 Performance in good faith An obligor has a duty to perform according to the requirements of good faith, taking customary practice into consideration. table of contents Section 243 Obligation described by class (1) A person who owes a thing defined only by class is to supply a thing of average kind and quality. (2) If the obligor has done what is necessary on their part to supply such a thing, then the obligation is restricted to that thing. table of contents Section 244 Foreign currency obligation (1) If a money debt stated in a currency other than the euro is payable within the territory of Germany, then payment may be made in euros unless payment in the other currency has been expressly agreed. (2) Conversion occurs at the rate of exchange in effect in the place of payment at the time of payment.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the original obligations, creating a contradiction under the defined perturbation type. The original text uses 'will,' indicating a clear obligation, while the modified text uses 'should,' introducing discretion. Furthermore, the changes from 'are taking place' to 'might be taking place', 'any other details' to 'certain other details' and 'any purpose' to 'purposes other than something related to' creates an ambiguity and reduces the scope of the restriction. § 241 (2) of the German Civil Code, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 2, states that the obligation can require each party to take account of the rights, legal interests and other interests of the other part; softening the obligation is a contradiction of those legal interests. The change creates an ambiguous legal obligation that is difficult to enforce and opens pathways for breaches to occur.

perturbation.original_text: The Client agrees to ensure that each of the Authorised Recipients adheres to the terms set forth in this NDA and acknowledges that it shall be liable for any damages incurred in connection with the breach of its obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Client agrees to encourage each of the Authorised Recipients to consider the terms set forth in this NDA and acknowledges that it may be exposed to liability for certain damages arguably connected with a potential breach of its obligations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to ensure' to 'agrees to encourage' significantly weakens the client's responsibility for the actions of Authorised Recipients. Also, changing 'shall be liable for any damages' to 'may be exposed to liability for certain damages arguably connected' introduces ambiguity regarding the extent and nature of liability.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 276 of the BGB deals with the responsibility of the obligor. It states that, unless otherwise specified, the obligor is responsible for willful intent and negligence. By changing "ensuring" to "encourage" and replacing the certainty of "shall be liable" with the possibility of "may be exposed to liability," the modified text introduces a lack of due care or diligence on the part of the obligor, which goes against the principle of reasonable care required under § 276 BGB.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 276 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 276 Verantwortlichkeit des Schuldners (1) Der Schuldner hat Vorsatz und Fahrlässigkeit zu vertreten, wenn eine strengere oder mildere Haftung weder bestimmt noch aus dem sonstigen Inhalt des Schuldverhältnisses, insbesondere aus der Übernahme einer Garantie oder eines Beschaffungsrisikos, zu entnehmen ist. Die Vorschriften der §§ 827 und 828 finden entsprechende Anwendung. (2) Fahrlässig handelt, wer die im Verkehr erforderliche Sorgfalt außer Acht lässt. (3) Die Haftung wegen Vorsatzes kann dem Schuldner nicht im Voraus erlassen werden. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant ambiguity regarding the Client's liability, potentially conflicting with the principle of reasonable care as outlined in § 276 BGB. The original text states the Client 'shall be liable for any damages,' establishing a clear obligation. The changed text softens this to 'may be exposed to liability for certain damages arguably connected,' weakening the obligation and introducing uncertainty. § 276 BGB states the debtor is responsible for intent and negligence unless stricter or milder liability is specified. The change from 'ensure' to 'encourage' and the introduction of 'may' and 'arguably connected' could be interpreted as attempting to specify a milder liability without explicitly doing so, which introduces ambiguity and reduces the client's responsibility, potentially conflicting with the expectation of reasonable care.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA shall be governed by, and construed in accordance of German law, and each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the German courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA should generally be regarded with some reference to German law, and each party considers submitting to the general jurisdiction of courts perhaps located in Germany.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be governed by' to 'should generally be regarded with some reference to' weakens the governing law clause. Also, replacing 'irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction' with 'considers submitting to the general jurisdiction of courts perhaps located in Germany' creates significant ambiguity about jurisdiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 202 of the BGB relates to the limitation of liability by prior agreement. German law typically favors clear and unambiguous agreements, especially regarding jurisdiction. The modifications introduce vagueness and uncertainty that could impede the clear establishment of jurisdiction, which could be interpreted as undermining the intent of the parties to clearly define their legal relationship and limit their obligations, and may be construed as a waiver of rights, which is invalid under German law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 202 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 202 Unzulässigkeit von Vereinbarungen über die Verjährung (1) Die Verjährung kann bei Haftung wegen Vorsatzes nicht im Voraus durch Rechtsgeschäft erleichtert werden. (2) Die Verjährung kann durch Rechtsgeschäft nicht über eine Verjährungsfrist von 30 Jahren ab dem gesetzlichen Verjährungsbeginn hinaus erschwert werden. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that the NDA 'shall be governed by' German law and that the parties 'irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the German courts.' This creates a clear and enforceable agreement on governing law and jurisdiction. The changed text, however, uses weaker language, stating that the NDA 'should generally be regarded with some reference to German law' and that the parties 'consider submitting to the general jurisdiction of courts perhaps located in Germany.' This creates ambiguity regarding the governing law and jurisdiction, making it difficult to enforce the agreement in German courts. The law explanation states German law favors clear and unambiguous agreements, especially regarding jurisdiction, which this change undermines.
